sizing_rule.note: Position size scaled by N(Z) — cumulative normal distribution probability derived from Z-score of historical trade data. Uses approximation formula for N(Z). Author (Vince) did not disclose optimal_f, kelly_fraction, or max_drawdown_tolerance parameters. Scaling factor is the probability output from N(Z), which ranges [0,1].

source_rule_text: Position sizing is determined by calculating the probability associated with a standard normal distribution value (Z-score) using the cumulative normal distribution function N(Z), which converts parametric data into probability-weighted position adjustments.
